overall_notes: Sentinel Records Ltd presents a high-quality, evidence-rich sustainability disclosure. All five dimensions are well-supported with quantified data, named standards, and third-party assurance, with board governance and executive pay linkage adding credibility to the transition plan. The only area short of the highest mark is third-party assurance, which is currently limited rather than reasonable assurance — a gap the company explicitly plans to close by 2026.

scores.dimension_name: Net Zero commitment and transition plan
scores.evidence_quote: Net Zero by 2045 commitment…interim milestones…published Carbon Reduction Plan (December 2024)…Board has oversight…Executive remuneration is partially linked
scores.scoring_rationale: Sentinel states a Net Zero by 2045 target with a validated near-term 2030 interim milestone (-50% Scope 1&2), a published Carbon Reduction Plan with four named decarbonisation levers and committed capex of £14.2m, and board-level accountability with executive pay linkage. All three criteria for score 3 are met.

scores.evidence_quote: Scope 1 (fuel, fleet, refrigerants)…2,440 [tCO2e]; Scope 2 location-based…4,025; Scope 2 market-based…780
scores.scoring_rationale: Both Scope 1 and Scope 2 (location-based and market-based) are disclosed in absolute tCO2e with a four-year time series (2019, 2022, 2023, 2024) including year-on-year percentage changes, and the data is covered by third-party limited assurance from Brackenbury Verification Services Ltd under ISAE 3000/3410.

scores.evidence_quote: We disclose Scope 3 emissions across all categories deemed material…7 are reported as material…Total material Scope 3 58,800 tCO2e
scores.scoring_rationale: Seven material Scope 3 categories are quantified in tCO2e with calculation methodologies documented per category; a materiality assessment methodology is referenced (Appendix B), and selected Scope 3 categories (1, 4, 6, 9) are independently assured under ISAE 3000/3410, meeting the criteria for comprehensive disclosure with methodology and third-party assurance.

scores.evidence_quote: Limited assurance by Brackenbury Verification Services Ltd…in accordance with ISAE 3000 (Revised) and ISAE 3410…full assurance statement published
scores.scoring_rationale: A named third-party assurer (Brackenbury Verification Services Ltd) has provided limited assurance to named standards (ISAE 3000 Revised and ISAE 3410), with the assurance statement published separately. This meets the score 2 criteria; however, the engagement is limited assurance rather than reasonable assurance, so score 3 is not yet warranted — the document itself notes reasonable assurance is targeted for 2026.

scores.evidence_quote: targets are validated by the Science Based Targets initiative against the Corporate Net-Zero Standard (near-term, validated November 2023)
scores.scoring_rationale: Near-term targets have been formally validated by SBTi against the Corporate Net-Zero Standard (November 2023), and quantified progress against those targets is reported in Section 5. Long-term target validation is noted as in progress, but the confirmed near-term validation with reported progress is sufficient for score 3.
